revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、[[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の...'
revision.text:
'''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、[[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。別氏とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。
[[奈良時代]][[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟の[[和気清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮]]神託に関する事件で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子[[和気広世|広世]]などは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している。以後は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけての医家半井家（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる。
==関連項目== *[[和気神社]] **[[和気神社 (霧島市)]] *[[護王神社]] *[[神護寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おのうし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}}

'''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、[[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。別氏とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。
[[奈良時代]][[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟の[[和気清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子[[和気広世|広世]]などは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している。以後は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけての医家半井家（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる。
==関連項目== *[[和気神社]] **[[和気神社 (霧島市)]] *[[護王神社]] *[[神護寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おのうし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}}

'''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、[[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。別氏とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。
[[奈良時代]][[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟の[[和気清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子[[和気広世|広世]]などは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している。以後は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官をつとめた医家半井家（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる<ref>難波恒雄「日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学」『和漢医薬学雑誌』1997年、14巻、p72</ref>。
==脚注== <references />
==関連項目== *[[和気神社]] **[[和気神社 (霧島市)]] *[[護王神社]] *[[神護寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}}

[[奈良時代]][[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟の[[和気清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子[[和気広世|広世]]などは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している。以後は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官をつとめた医家半井家（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる<ref>難波恒雄「日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学」『和漢医薬学雑誌』1997年、14巻、p72</ref>。

'''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、[[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。別氏とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。
[[奈良時代]][[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟の[[和気清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子[[和気広世|広世]]などは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している<ref>ただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている。久木『日本古代学校の研究』（1990年、玉川大学出版部）</ref>。以後は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官をつとめた医家半井家（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる<ref>難波恒雄「日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学」『和漢医薬学雑誌』1997年、14巻、p72</ref>。

== 概要 == [[奈良時代]]の[[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟の[[和気清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子[[和気広世|広世]]などは学者として[[朝廷]]に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置しているただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている。久木『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）。以後は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官をつとめた医家[[半井家]]（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる難波恒雄「日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学」『和漢医薬学雑誌』[[1997年]]、14巻、p72。 ==脚注== ==関連項目== * [[和気神社]] ** [[和気神社 (霧島市)]] * [[護王神社]] * [[神護寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:和気氏|*]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[category:本姓]] [[Category:典薬寮|氏わけ]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}}

[[藤木家]]（[[地下家]]）
[[小森家]]（[[地下家]]）
[[多田家]]（[[地下家]]）
清水家（[[地下家]]）
[[兼田家]]（[[地下家]]）
[[小畠家]]（[[地下家]]） など }} '''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、「和気」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 [[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の[[皇子]]鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。「別氏」とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。 __TOC__
== 概要 == [[奈良時代]]の[[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟の[[和気清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子[[和気広世|広世]]などは学者として[[朝廷]]に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置しているただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている。久木『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）。 和気氏の一門からは[[清和源氏|源氏]]の[[棟梁]] [[源頼義]]に仕えた[[和気致輔]]が出るなど、武士となった者も現れた。 和気氏の家系は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官をつとめた医家[[半井家]]（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる難波恒雄「日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学」『和漢医薬学雑誌』[[1997年]]、14巻、p72。 ==脚注== ==関連項目== * [[和気神社]] ** [[和気神社 (霧島市)]] * [[護王神社]] * [[神護寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:和気氏|*]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[category:氏]] [[Category:典薬寮|氏わけ]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}}

[[小畠家]]（[[地下家]]） など }} '''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、「和気」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 [[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の[[皇子]]鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。「別氏」とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公 (カバネ)|公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。 __TOC__ {{clear}} == 概要 == [[奈良時代]]の[[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟の[[和気清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子[[和気広世|広世]]などは学者として[[朝廷]]に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置しているただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている。久木『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）。 和気氏の一門からは[[清和源氏|源氏]]の[[棟梁]] [[源頼義]]に仕えた[[和気致輔]]が出るなど、武士となった者も現れた。 和気氏の家系は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官をつとめた医家[[半井家]]（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる難波恒雄「日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学」『和漢医薬学雑誌』[[1997年]]、14巻、p72。 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[和気神社]] ** [[和気神社 (霧島市)]] * [[護王神社]] * [[神護寺]] * [[和気 (曖昧さ回避)|和気]] {{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:和気氏|*]] [[category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:中国地方の氏族]] [[Category:典薬寮|氏わけ]] [[Category:和気郡]] [[Category:磐梨郡]] [[Category:和気町の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}}

[[浜崎氏]](武家)など }} '''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、「和気」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 [[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子・鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。「別氏」とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公 (カバネ)|公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。 __TOC__ {{clear}} == 概要 == [[奈良時代]]の[[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟・[[和気清麻呂|清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子・[[和気広世|広世]]らは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している{{Efn|ただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている{{Sfn|久木|1990|p={{要ページ番号|date=2018年10月}}}}。}}。 和気氏の一門からは[[清和源氏|源氏]]の[[棟梁]] [[源頼義]]に仕えた[[和気致輔]]が出るなど、武士となった者も現れた。 和気氏の家系は医家として仕え、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官を務めた医家[[半井家]]（なからいけ）は和気氏の子孫とされる{{Sfn|難波|1997|p=72}}。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|last=久木|first=幸男|author-link=久木幸男|date=1990-7|title=日本古代学校の研究|publisher=玉川大学出版部|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=難波恒雄|authorlink=難波恒雄|title=日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学|date=1997|publisher=和漢医薬学会|journal=和漢医薬学雑誌|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|難波|1997}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[和気神社]] ** [[和気神社 (霧島市)]] * [[護王神社]] * [[神護寺]] *[[広虫神社]]―広島県尾道市御調町にある[[御調八幡宮]]内に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[和気広虫]]。 * [[和気 (曖昧さ回避)|和気]] *[[半井小絵]]―元NHK気象キャスター。本人が自身の家を和気氏の末裔・半井氏であると発言している。 {{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:和気氏|*]] [[category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:中国地方の氏族]] [[Category:典薬寮|氏わけ]] [[Category:和気郡]] [[Category:磐梨郡]] [[Category:和気町の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}}

{{出典の明記|date=2017年6月}} {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 和気氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 和気[[公]]

[[浜崎氏]](武家)など }} '''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、「和気」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 [[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子・鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。「別氏」とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公 (カバネ)|公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。 == 概要 == [[奈良時代]]の[[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟・[[和気清麻呂|清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子・[[和気広世|広世]]らは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している{{Efn|ただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている{{Sfn|久木|1990|p={{要ページ番号|date=2018年10月}}}}。}}。 清麻呂の五男[[和気真綱|真綱]]は[[参議]]まで昇進したが、その後は[[公卿]]を出すこともなく[[地下家]]として朝廷に仕えた。ただし[[即位]]や[[朔旦冬至]]の際に[[氏爵]]を受ける特権を保持しており、戦国時代頃までその風習は続いた。 和気氏の系統を引く地下家の中で著名なのは[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官を務めた医家[[半井家]]（なからいけ）がある{{Sfn|難波|1997|p=72}}。江戸時代には半井萬助が和漢薬業の「半井萬助商店」を創業し、その後身は試薬メーカーの[[ナカライテスク]]となっている。また[[清和源氏|源氏]]の[[棟梁]]・[[源頼義]]に仕えた[[和気致輔]]が出るなど、武士となった者も現れた。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|last=久木|first=幸男|author-link=久木幸男|date=1990-7|title=日本古代学校の研究|publisher=玉川大学出版部|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=難波恒雄|authorlink=難波恒雄|title=日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学|date=1997|publisher=和漢医薬学会|journal=和漢医薬学雑誌|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|難波|1997}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[和気神社]] ** [[和気神社 (霧島市)]] * [[護王神社]] * [[神護寺]] *[[広虫神社]]―広島県尾道市御調町にある[[御調八幡宮]]内に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[和気広虫]]。 * [[和気 (曖昧さ回避)|和気]] *[[半井小絵]]―元NHK気象キャスター。本人が自身の家を和気氏の末裔・半井氏であると発言している。 {{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:和気氏|*]] [[category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:中国地方の氏族]] [[Category:典薬寮|氏わけ]] [[Category:和気郡]] [[Category:磐梨郡]] [[Category:和気町の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}}

[[浜崎氏]](武家)など }} '''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、「和気」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 [[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子・鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。「別氏」とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公 (カバネ)|公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。 == 概要 == [[奈良時代]]の[[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟・[[和気清麻呂|清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子・[[和気広世|広世]]らは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している{{Efn|ただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている{{Sfn|久木|1990|p={{要ページ番号|date=2018年10月}}}}。}}。 清麻呂の五男[[和気真綱|真綱]]は[[参議]]まで昇進したが、その後は[[公卿]]を出すこともなく[[地下家]]として朝廷に仕えた。ただし[[即位]]や[[朔旦冬至]]の際に[[氏爵]]を受ける特権を保持しており、戦国時代頃までその風習は続いた。 和気氏の系統を引く地下家の中で著名なのは[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官を務めた医家[[半井家]]（なからいけ）がある{{Sfn|難波|1997|p=72}}。江戸時代には半井萬助が和漢薬業の「半井萬助商店」を創業し、その後身は試薬メーカーの[[ナカライテスク]]となっている。また[[清和源氏|源氏]]の[[棟梁]]・[[源頼義]]に仕えた[[和気致輔]]が出るなど、武士となった者も現れた。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|last=久木|first=幸男|author-link=久木幸男|date=1990-7|title=日本古代学校の研究|publisher=玉川大学出版部|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=難波恒雄|authorlink=難波恒雄|title=日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学|date=1997|publisher=和漢医薬学会|journal=和漢医薬学雑誌|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|難波|1997}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[和気神社]] ** [[和気神社 (霧島市)]] * [[護王神社]] * [[神護寺]] *[[広虫神社]]―広島県尾道市御調町にある[[御調八幡宮]]内に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[和気広虫]]。 * [[和気 (曖昧さ回避)|和気]] *[[半井小絵]]―元NHK気象キャスター。本人が自身の家を和気氏の末裔・半井氏であると発言している。 {{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:和気氏|*]] [[category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:中国地方の氏族]] [[Category:典薬寮|氏わけ]] [[Category:和気郡]] [[Category:磐梨郡]] [[Category:和気町の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}}

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|Nakarai Tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}}

One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}}

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}}

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}}

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}}

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]]

They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wake clan]][[Category:11]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]]

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]]

Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]]
