The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]]

Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]]

They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]]

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]]

The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}} [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ake clan]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%92%8C%E6%B0%97%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Wake clan]] [[en:Wake clan]] [[sn:ake clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}}

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|12=draft}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国|12=draft}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=draft}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂|12=draft}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=draft}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡|12=draft}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=draft}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|12=draft}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|12=draft}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館|12=draft}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|12=draft}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議|12=draft}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=draft}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=draft}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge|12=draft}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=draft}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=draft}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=draft}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義|12=draft}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏|12=draft}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=draft}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=draft}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%92%8C%E6%B0%97%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Wake clan]] [[en:Wake clan]] [[sn:ake clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}}

Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=draft}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=draft}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義|12=draft}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏|12=draft}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=draft}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=draft}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%92%8C%E6%B0%97%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Wake clan]] [[en:Wake clan]] [[sn:ake clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}}

{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|12=draft}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|ja|和気郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国|12=draft}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ({{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=draft}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂|12=draft}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=draft}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡|12=draft}}. As a result, they rose in rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=draft}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|12=draft}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|12=draft}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|library|ja|図書館|12=draft}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|12=draft}}, rose to the rank of {{ill|Sangi (councilor)|ja|参議|12=draft}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=draft}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=draft}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge|12=draft}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=draft}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=draft}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=draft}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼義|12=draft}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏|12=draft}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine|ja|護王神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|ja|神護寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=draft}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Onomichi, Hiroshima|ja|尾道市|12=draft}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=draft}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Wake clan|*]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:Tenyakuryō|氏 Wake]] [[Category:Wake District, Okayama]] [[Category:Iwanari District]] [[Category:Wake, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%92%8C%E6%B0%97%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Wake clan]] [[en:Wake clan]] [[sn:ake clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wake clan}}

{{出典の明記|date=2017年6月}} {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 和気氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 和気[[公]]（磐梨別公、藤野別公、別公）
和気[[宿禰]]（石成別宿禰、石成宿禰）
和気[[真人]]（藤野別真人、輔治能真人）
和気[[朝臣]] |始祖 = |出自 = |氏祖 = [[鐸石別命]]
（[[垂仁天皇]]皇子） |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = [[備前国]][[和気郡]] |人物 = [[和気広虫]]
[[和気清麻呂]]
[[円珍]] |後裔 = [[半井家]]（[[地下家]]）
[[駒井家]]（地下家）
[[藤木家]]（地下家）
[[小森家]]（地下家）
[[多田家]]（地下家）
清水家（地下家）
[[兼田家]]（地下家）
[[小畠家]]（地下家）
[[浜崎氏]](武家)など }} '''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、「和気」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 [[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子・鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。「別氏」とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公 (カバネ)|公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。 == 概要 == [[奈良時代]]の[[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟・[[和気清麻呂|清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子・[[和気広世|広世]]らは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している{{Efn|ただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている{{Sfn|久木|1990|p={{要ページ番号|date=2018年10月}}}}。}}。 清麻呂の五男[[和気真綱|真綱]]は[[参議]]まで昇進したが、その後は[[公卿]]を出すこともなく[[地下家]]として朝廷に仕えた。ただし[[即位]]や[[朔旦冬至]]の際に[[氏爵]]を受ける特権を保持しており、戦国時代頃までその風習は続いた。 和気氏の系統を引く地下家の中で著名なのは[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官を務めた医家[[半井家]]（なからいけ）がある{{Sfn|難波|1997|p=72}}。江戸時代には半井萬助が和漢薬業の「半井萬助商店」を創業し、その後身は試薬メーカーの[[ナカライテスク]]となっている。また[[清和源氏|源氏]]の[[棟梁]]・[[源頼義]]に仕えた[[和気致輔]]が出るなど、武士となった者も現れた。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|last=久木|first=幸男|author-link=久木幸男|date=1990-7|title=日本古代学校の研究|publisher=玉川大学出版部|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=難波恒雄|authorlink=難波恒雄|title=日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学|date=1997|publisher=和漢医薬学会|journal=和漢医薬学雑誌|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|難波|1997}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[和気神社]] ** [[和気神社 (霧島市)]] * [[護王神社]] * [[神護寺]] * [[広虫神社]] - [[広島県]][[尾道市]]にある[[御調八幡宮]]内に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[和気広虫]]。 * [[半井小絵]] - 元[[日本放送協会|NHK]]気象キャスター。本人が自身の家を和気氏の末裔・半井氏であると発言している。 {{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:和気氏|*]] [[category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:中国地方の氏族]] [[Category:典薬寮|氏わけ]] [[Category:和気郡]] [[Category:磐梨郡]] [[Category:和気町の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}}

[[小畠家]]（地下家）
[[浜崎氏]](武家)など }} '''和気氏'''（わけうじ）は、「和気」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。 [[備前国]][[和気郡]]（古くは藤野郡と称した）を本拠とした[[豪族]]である。[[垂仁天皇]]の皇子・鐸石別命（ぬてしわけのみこと）を祖とすると伝えられる。「別氏」とも書く。[[姓]]（かばね）は[[公 (カバネ)|公]]（きみ）から[[真人]]（まひと）、[[宿禰]]などを経て[[774年]]（[[宝亀]]5年）に[[朝臣]]の姓が与えられた。 == 概要 == [[奈良時代]]の[[和気広虫]]（[[出家]]して法均尼と号する）と弟・[[和気清麻呂|清麻呂]]が僧[[道鏡]]に対する[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|宇佐八幡宮神託に関する事件]]で功があって[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]に進出し、藤野別真人から和気朝臣の氏姓が与えられた。清麻呂の子・[[和気広世|広世]]らは学者として朝廷に仕え、広世は和気氏出身者のための[[大学別曹]]の[[弘文院]]を設置している{{Efn|ただし、[[久木幸男]]は弘文院が大学別曹の条件と矛盾する要素を有しており、大学別曹ではなく[[図書館]]であったとする説を唱えている{{Sfn|久木|1990|p={{要ページ番号|date=2018年10月}}}}。}}。 清麻呂の五男[[和気真綱|真綱]]は[[参議]]まで昇進したが、その後は[[公卿]]を出すこともなく[[地下家]]として朝廷に仕えた。ただし[[即位]]や[[朔旦冬至]]の際に[[氏爵]]を受ける特権を保持しており、戦国時代頃までその風習は続いた。 和気氏の系統を引く地下家の中で著名なのは[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて代々宮廷医官を務めた医家[[半井家]]（なからいけ）がある{{Sfn|難波|1997|p=72}}。江戸時代には半井萬助が和漢薬業の「半井萬助商店」を創業し、その後身は試薬メーカーの[[ナカライテスク]]となっている。また[[清和源氏|源氏]]の[[棟梁]]・[[源頼義]]に仕えた[[和気致輔]]が出るなど、武士となった者も現れた。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|last=久木|first=幸男|author-link=久木幸男|date=1990-7|title=日本古代学校の研究|publisher=玉川大学出版部|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=難波恒雄|authorlink=難波恒雄|title=日本における本草の歴史と民族薬物学|date=1997|publisher=和漢医薬学会|journal=和漢医薬学雑誌|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|難波|1997}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[和気神社]] ** [[和気神社 (霧島市)]] * [[護王神社]] * [[神護寺]] * [[広虫神社]] - [[広島県]][[尾道市]]にある[[御調八幡宮]]内に鎮座する神社。祭神は[[和気広虫]]。 * [[半井小絵]] - 元[[日本放送協会|NHK]]気象キャスター。本人が自身の家を和気氏の末裔・半井氏であると発言している。 {{DEFAULTSORT:わけうし}} [[Category:和気氏|*]] [[category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:中国地方の氏族]] [[Category:典薬寮|氏わけ]] [[Category:和気郡]] [[Category:磐梨郡]] [[Category:和気町の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=岡山県}}

Wake [[Asomi|Asomi]] | founder = | origin = | clan ancestor = {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|qq=}}
(Son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]) | type = [[Kōbetsu|Imperial descent]] | home province = [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]], [[:en:Wake District, Okayama|Wake District, Okayama]] | notable people = {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}}
[[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]]

{{ill|Komai family|ja|駒井家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komai family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Fujiki family|ja|藤木家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiki family|qq=}} (Chikage)

{{ill|Tada family|ja|多田家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada family|qq=}} (Chikage)
Shimizu family (Chikage)
{{ill|Kaneda family|ja|兼田家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaneda family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Kobata family|ja|小畠家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|qq=}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in [[:en:Wake District, Okayama|Wake District, Okayama]], [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]], which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ([[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]] gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|qq=}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|qq=}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|qq=}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|qq=}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a [[:en:Library|library]] rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|qq=}}, rose to the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|qq=}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|qq=}}, a line of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|qq=}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|qq=}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|qq=}}, who served under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], a leader of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]]. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|qq=}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|qq=}} * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goryō Shrine]] * [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|qq=}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Onomichi|Onomichi, Hiroshima]], dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|qq=}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%92%8C%E6%B0%97%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Wake clan]] [[en:Wake clan]] [[sn:ake clan]]

(Son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]) | type = [[Kōbetsu|Imperial descent]] | home province = [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]], [[:en:Wake District, Okayama|Wake District, Okayama]] | notable people = {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}}
[[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]]
[[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] | cadet branches = {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komai family|ja|駒井家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komai family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Fujiki family|ja|藤木家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiki family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komori family|ja|小森家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Tada family|ja|多田家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada family|qq=}} (Chikage)
Shimizu family (Chikage)
{{ill|Kaneda family|ja|兼田家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaneda family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Kobata family|ja|小畠家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata family|qq=}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|qq=}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in [[:en:Wake District, Okayama|Wake District, Okayama]], [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]], which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ([[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]] gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|qq=}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|qq=}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|qq=}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|qq=}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a [[:en:Library|library]] rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|qq=}}, rose to the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|qq=}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|qq=}}, a line of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|qq=}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|qq=}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|qq=}}, who served under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], a leader of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]]. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|qq=}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|qq=}} * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goryō Shrine]] * [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|qq=}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Onomichi|Onomichi, Hiroshima]], dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|qq=}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%92%8C%E6%B0%97%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Wake clan]] [[en:Wake clan]] [[sn:ake clan]] [[Category:中国地方の氏族]] [[Category:典薬寮]]

Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|qq=}}, who served under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], a leader of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]]. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|qq=}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|qq=}} * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goryō Shrine]] * [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|qq=}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Onomichi|Onomichi, Hiroshima]], dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|qq=}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. {{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|qq=}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in [[:en:Wake District, Okayama|Wake District, Okayama]], [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]], which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[774]] ([[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]] gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|qq=}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|qq=}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|qq=}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|qq=}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a [[:en:Library|library]] rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|qq=}}, rose to the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|qq=}}, a privilege that continued until the [[Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|qq=}}, a line of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|qq=}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|qq=}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|qq=}}, who served under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], a leader of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]]. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|qq=}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|qq=}} * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goryō Shrine]] * [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|qq=}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Onomichi|Onomichi, Hiroshima]], dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|qq=}} – Former [[NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Chugoku region clans]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wake clan]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E5%92%8C%E6%B0%97%E6%B0%8F]] [[sn:Wake clan]] [[en:Wake clan]] [[sn:ake clan]] [[Category:中国地方の氏族]] [[Category:典薬寮]] [[Category:和気氏]] [[Category:和気町の歴史]] [[Category:和気郡]]
