[[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11417729]] Wikidata name: Wake clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clans | surname = Wake clan | image = | surname with titles = Wake [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (Iwanariwake Kimi, Fujinowake Kimi, Wake Kimi)
Wake [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (Ishinariwake Sukune, Ishinari Sukune)
Wake [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (Fujinowake Mahito, Fujino Mahito)
Wake [[Asomi|Asomi]] | founder = | origin = | clan ancestor = {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
(Son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]) | type = [[Kōbetsu|Imperial descent]] | home province = [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]], [[:en:Wake District, Okayama|Wake District, Okayama]] | notable people = {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}
[[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]]
[[:en:Enchin|Enchin]] | cadet branches = {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komai family|ja|駒井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Fujiki family|ja|藤木家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komori family|ja|小森家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Tada family|ja|多田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
Shimizu family (Chikage)
{{ill|Kaneda family|ja|兼田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaneda family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Kobata family|ja|小畠家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濱崎氏|1=Hamasaki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109604131}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that bore the name "Wake" as their [[:en:Uji|family name]]. The clan was a powerful local family based in [[:en:Wake District, Okayama|Wake District, Okayama]], [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]], which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in [[:en:774|774]] ([[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]] gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]]. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|1=Wake no hiroyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417723}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|1=Kōbun-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487348}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|1=Yukio Hisaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369404}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a [[:en:Library|library]] rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|1=Wake no Matsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417735}}, rose to the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬至|1=winter solstice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207932}}, a privilege that continued until the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}}, a line of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|1=Nacalai Tesque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11323705}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], a leader of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]]. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417736}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417737}} * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goryō Shrine]] * [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|1=Mitsuki Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489327}} in [[:en:Onomichi|Onomichi, Hiroshima]], dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|1=Sae Nakarai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406975}} – Former [[:en:NHK|NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:和気氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11417729}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Waki Town]] [[Category:Imperial Bureau of Pharmacology]] [[Category:Iwanashi District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Waki District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|1=Wake no hiroyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417723}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬至|1=winter solstice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207932}}, a privilege that continued until the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]]. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}}, a line of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|1=Nacalai Tesque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11323705}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], a leader of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]]. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417736}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417737}} * [[:en:Goō Shrine|Goryō Shrine]] * [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|1=Mitsuki Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489327}} in [[:en:Onomichi|Onomichi, Hiroshima]], dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|1=Sae Nakarai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406975}} – Former [[:en:NHK|NHK]] weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:和気氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11417729}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Waki Town]] [[Category:Imperial Bureau of Pharmacology]] [[Category:Iwanashi District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Waki District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11417729]] Wikidata name: Wake clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clans | surname = Wake clan | image = | surname with titles = Wake [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (Iwanariwake Kimi, Fujinowake Kimi, Wake Kimi)
Wake [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (Ishinariwake Sukune, Ishinari Sukune)
Wake [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (Fujinowake Mahito, Fujino Mahito)
Wake [[Asomi|Asomi]] | founder = | origin = | clan ancestor = {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
(Son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}) | type = [[Kōbetsu|Imperial descent]] | home province = {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}, {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama}} | notable people = {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}
{{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro}}
{{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin}} | cadet branches = {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komai family|ja|駒井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Fujiki family|ja|藤木家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komori family|ja|小森家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Tada family|ja|多田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
Shimizu family (Chikage)
{{ill|Kaneda family|ja|兼田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaneda family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Kobata family|ja|小畠家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濱崎氏|1=Hamasaki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109604131}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan}} that bore the name "Wake" as their {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=family name}}. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in {{ill|774|en|774|WD=Q30528|lt=774}} ({{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki era}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō}}. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|1=Wake no hiroyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417723}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|1=Kōbun-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487348}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|1=Yukio Hisaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369404}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|Library|en|Library|WD=Q7075|lt=library}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|1=Wake no Matsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417735}}, rose to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬至|1=winter solstice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207932}}, a privilege that continued until the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|1=Nacalai Tesque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11323705}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417736}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417737}} * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goryō Shrine}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|1=Mitsuki Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489327}} in {{ill|Onomichi|en|Onomichi|WD=Q696694|lt=Onomichi, Hiroshima}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|1=Sae Nakarai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406975}} – Former {{ill|NHK|en|NHK|WD=Q212128|lt=NHK}} weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:和気氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11417729}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Waki Town]] [[Category:Imperial Bureau of Pharmacology]] [[Category:Iwanashi District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Waki District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11417729]] Wikidata name: Wake clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clans | surname = Wake clan | image = | surname with titles = Wake [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (Iwanariwake Kimi, Fujinowake Kimi, Wake Kimi)
Wake [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (Ishinariwake Sukune, Ishinari Sukune)
Wake [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (Fujinowake Mahito, Fujino Mahito)
Wake [[Asomi|Asomi]] | founder = | origin = | clan ancestor = {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
(Son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}) | type = [[Kōbetsu|Imperial descent]] | home province = {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}, {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama}} | notable people = {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}
{{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro}}
{{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin}} | cadet branches = {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komai family|ja|駒井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Fujiki family|ja|藤木家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komori family|ja|小森家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Tada family|ja|多田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
Shimizu family (Chikage)
{{ill|Kaneda family|ja|兼田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaneda family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Kobata family|ja|小畠家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濱崎氏|1=Hamasaki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109604131}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan}} that bore the name "Wake" as their {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=family name}}. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in {{ill|774|en|774|WD=Q30528|lt=774}} ({{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki era}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō}}. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|1=Wake no hiroyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417723}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|1=Kōbun-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487348}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|1=Yukio Hisaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369404}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|Library|en|Library|WD=Q7075|lt=library}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|1=Wake no Matsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417735}}, rose to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬至|1=winter solstice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207932}}, a privilege that continued until the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|1=Nacalai Tesque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11323705}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}}. == References == === Notes {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|1=Mitsuki Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489327}} in {{ill|Onomichi|en|Onomichi|WD=Q696694|lt=Onomichi, Hiroshima}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|1=Sae Nakarai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406975}} – Former {{ill|NHK|en|NHK|WD=Q212128|lt=NHK}} weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:和気氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11417729}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Waki Town]] [[Category:Imperial Bureau of Pharmacology]] [[Category:Iwanashi District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Waki District]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] {{Refimprove|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clans | surname = Wake clan | image = | surname with titles = Wake [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (Iwanariwake Kimi, Fujinowake Kimi, Wake Kimi)
Wake [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (Ishinariwake Sukune, Ishinari Sukune)
Wake [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (Fujinowake Mahito, Fujino Mahito)
Wake [[Asomi|Asomi]] | founder = | origin = | clan ancestor = {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
(Son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}) | type = [[Kōbetsu|Imperial descent]] | home province = {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}, {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama}} | notable people = {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}
{{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro}}
{{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin}} | cadet branches = {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komai family|ja|駒井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Fujiki family|ja|藤木家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komori family|ja|小森家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Tada family|ja|多田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
Shimizu family (Chikage)
{{ill|Kaneda family|ja|兼田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaneda family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Kobata family|ja|小畠家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濱崎氏|1=Hamasaki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109604131}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan}} that bore the name "Wake" as their {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=family name}}. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in {{ill|774|en|774|WD=Q30528|lt=774}} ({{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki era}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō}}. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|1=Wake no hiroyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417723}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|1=Kōbun-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487348}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|1=Yukio Hisaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369404}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|Library|en|Library|WD=Q7075|lt=library}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|1=Wake no Matsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417735}}, rose to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬至|1=winter solstice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207932}}, a privilege that continued until the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|1=Nacalai Tesque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11323705}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417736}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417737}} * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goryō Shrine}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|1=Mitsuki Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489327}} in {{ill|Onomichi|en|Onomichi|WD=Q696694|lt=Onomichi, Hiroshima}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|1=Sae Nakarai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406975}} – Former {{ill|NHK|en|NHK|WD=Q212128|lt=NHK}} weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:和気氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11417729}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Waki Town]] [[Category:Imperial Bureau of Pharmacology]] [[Category:Iwanashi District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Waki District]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|1=Sae Nakarai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406975}} – Former {{ill|NHK|en|NHK|WD=Q212128|lt=NHK}} weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:和気氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11417729}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Waki Town]] [[Category:Imperial Bureau of Pharmacology]] [[Category:Iwanashi District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Waki District]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] [[ja:和気氏]] {{Refimprove|date=June 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clans | surname = Wake clan | image = | surname with titles = Wake [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (Iwanariwake Kimi, Fujinowake Kimi, Wake Kimi)
Wake [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (Ishinariwake Sukune, Ishinari Sukune)
Wake [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (Fujinowake Mahito, Fujino Mahito)
Wake [[Asomi|Asomi]] | founder = | origin = | clan ancestor = {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
(Son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}) | type = [[Kōbetsu|Imperial descent]] | home province = {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}, {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama|1=Wake District, Okayama}} | notable people = {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}
{{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}
{{ill|Enchin|en|Enchin|WD=Q1972505|lt=Enchin|1=Enchin}} | cadet branches = {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komai family|ja|駒井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komai family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Fujiki family|ja|藤木家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiki family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Komori family|ja|小森家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Tada family|ja|多田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
Shimizu family (Chikage)
{{ill|Kaneda family|ja|兼田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaneda family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Kobata family|ja|小畠家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濱崎氏|1=Q109604131|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109604131}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} that bore the name "Wake" as their {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=family name|1=Uji}}. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama|1=Wake District, Okayama}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in {{ill|774|en|774|WD=Q30528|lt=774|1=774}} ({{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki era|1=Hōki}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō|1=Dōkyō}}. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|1=Wake no hiroyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417723}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|1=Kōbun-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487348}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|1=Yukio Hisaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369404}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|Library|en|Library|WD=Q7075|lt=library|1=Library}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|1=Wake no Matsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417735}}, rose to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬至|1=Winter solstice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207932}}, a privilege that continued until the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}.{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|1=Nacalai Tesque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11323705}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}}. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Citations === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation|和書|last=Kuki|first=Hisao|author-link=Hisao Kuki|date=July 1990|title=Research on Ancient Japanese Schools|publisher=Tamagawa University Press|isbn=447207981X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite journal |和書|author=Namba Tsuneo|authorlink=Namba Tsuneo|title=The History of Japanese Herbal Medicine and Ethnopharmacology|date=1997|publisher=Japanese Society of Pharmacognosy|journal=Journal of Japanese Pharmacognosy|volume=14|issue=|issn=13406302|pages=|ref={{SfnRef|Namba|1997}}}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Wake Shrine|ja|和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417736}} ** {{ill|Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|ja|和気神社 (霧島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Shrine (Kirishima)|1=Wake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417737}} * {{ill|Goō Shrine|en|Goō Shrine|WD=Q11632526|lt=Goryō Shrine|1=Goō Shrine}} * {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji|1=Jingo-ji}} * {{ill|Hiromushi Shrine|ja|広虫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Located within {{ill|Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|ja|御調八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsugi Hachiman Shrine|1=Mitsuki Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489327}} in {{ill|Onomichi|en|Onomichi|WD=Q696694|lt=Onomichi, Hiroshima|1=Onomichi}}, dedicated to {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}. * {{ill|Sae Nakarai|ja|半井小絵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sae Nakarai|1=Sae Nakarai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406975}} – Former {{ill|NHK|en|NHK|WD=Q212128|lt=NHK|1=NHK}} weathercaster. She has stated that her family, the Nakarai, are descendants of the Wake clan. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Okayama Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|和気氏|version=99713213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:和気氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11417729}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Waki Town]] [[Category:Imperial Bureau of Pharmacology]] [[Category:Iwanashi District]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wake clan]] [[Category:Waki District]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

(Son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}) | type = [[Kōbetsu|Imperial descent]] | home province = {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}, {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama|1=Wake District, Okayama}} | notable people = {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}}

Shimizu family (Chikage)
{{ill|Kaneda family|ja|兼田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaneda family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Kobata family|ja|小畠家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chikage)
{{ill|Hamasaki clan|ja|浜崎氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濱崎氏|1=Q109604131|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109604131}} (Samurai) and others }} The '''Wake clan''' (和気氏) was a Japanese {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} that bore the name "Wake" as their {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=family name|1=Uji}}. The clan was a powerful local family based in {{ill|Wake District, Okayama|en|Wake District, Okayama|WD=Q1155355|lt=Wake District, Okayama|1=Wake District, Okayama}}, {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province|1=Bizen Province}}, which was historically called "Fujino District." They claimed descent from {{ill|Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja|鐸石別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuteshiwake-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The name was also sometimes written as "Betsu clan" (別氏). Their noble titles ({{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) changed over time: they held the title [[Kimi (kabane)|Kimi]] (公), later becoming [[Mahito (kabane)|Mahito]] (真人), [[Sukune (kabane)|Sukune]] (宿禰), and finally, in {{ill|774|en|774|WD=Q30528|lt=774|1=774}} ({{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki era|1=Hōki}} 5), they were granted the title [[Asomi|Asomi]] (朝臣). == Overview == During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, {{ill|Wake no Hiromushi|ja|和気広虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiromushi|1=Wake no Hiromushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10921594}} (who later became a Buddhist nun and took the name Hōkinni) and her younger brother {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}} gained prominence due to their role in the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, which involved resisting the influence of the Buddhist monk {{ill|Dōkyō|en|Dōkyō|WD=Q1272718|lt=Dōkyō|1=Dōkyō}}. As a result, they rose in Rank within the [[:en:Imperial court|Imperial court]] and were granted the clan title of "Wake Asomi" (和気朝臣) from their previous title "Fujinowake Mahito" (藤野別真人). Kiyomaro’s son, {{ill|Wake no Hiroyo|ja|和気広世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Hiroyo|1=Wake no hiroyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417723}}, and other descendants became scholars and served the court. Hiroyo established the {{ill|Kōbun-in|ja|弘文院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbun-in|1=Kōbun-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487348}}, a special educational institution for members of the Wake clan, considered one of the [[Daigaku betsu-sō|private academies for aristocratic families]]{{efn|However, scholar {{ill|Hisao Kuki|ja|久木幸男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Kuki|1=Yukio Hisaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369404}} has argued that Kōbun-in functioned more as a {{ill|Library|en|Library|WD=Q7075|lt=library|1=Library}} rather than an academy.{{sfn|Kuki|1990|p={{page needed|date=October 2018}}}}}}. Kiyomaro's fifth son, {{ill|Wake no Masatsuna|ja|和気真綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Masatsuna|1=Wake no Matsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417735}}, rose to the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, but later generations of the Wake clan did not achieve high court Ranks. Instead, they remained among the {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=地下家|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}, a class of non-aristocratic court families. Despite this, they retained the privilege of receiving [[clan Ranks]] (氏爵) during imperial successions and events like {{ill|Sokutan tōji|ja|朔旦冬至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokutan tōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冬至|1=Winter solstice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207932}}, a privilege that continued until the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}. One of the most prominent families descended from the Wake clan was the {{ill|Nakarai family|ja|半井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai family|1=Nakarai family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121295088}}, a line of {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} court physicians who served the imperial family from the Sengoku period into the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}.{{sfn|Namba|1997|p=72}} During the Edo period, {{ill|Nakarai Mansuke|ja|半井萬助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakarai Mansuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established "Nakarai Mansuke Shoten," which later evolved into the pharmaceutical company {{ill|nacalai tesque|ja|ナカライテスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nacalai tesque|1=Nacalai Tesque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11323705}}. Some members of the Wake clan also became samurai, such as {{ill|Wake no Chikashige|ja|和気致輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Chikashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}}, a leader of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa
